revision.comment: [[WP:AES|←]]新しいページ: ''''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石...'
revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。
== 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 == * [[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]） * [[牽牛子塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[高松塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[キトラ古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[中尾山古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[石のカラト古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]・[[京都府]][[木津川市]]） * [[シシヨツカ古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） * [[小口山古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） * [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[お亀石古墳]]（大阪府[[富田林市]]） * [[向山古墳群#主な古墳|岩屋古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） * [[団原古墳]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]） * [[古宮古墳]]（[[大分県]][[大分市]]）
== ギャラリー == <gallery widths="180px" heights="150px"> 画像:Oni no Secchin.JPG|鬼の雪隠 File:観音塚古墳（羽曳野市）石室内部.JPG|観音塚古墳石室内部 画像:向山1号墳玄室.JPG|岩屋古墳石室内部 ファイル:Danbara kofun-sekishitsu.jpg|伝・団原古墳出土石室 </gallery>
== 参考文献 == * [[田村晃一]]・合田芳正（監修）『考古学探訪の基礎用語』[[山川出版社]]、141頁、2000年7月
== 関連項目 == * [[古墳]] * [[石棺]] * [[石室]] * [[益田岩船]] * [[終末期古墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:よこあなしきせつかく}} [[Category:考古遺跡に見る葬制]] [[Category:古墳]]

revision.comment: /* 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 */
revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。
== 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 == * [[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]） * [[牽牛子塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[高松塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[キトラ古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[中尾山古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[束明神古墳]]（奈良県高取町） * [[石のカラト古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]・[[京都府]][[木津川市]]） * [[シシヨツカ古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） * [[小口山古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） * [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[お亀石古墳]]（大阪府[[富田林市]]） * [[向山古墳群#主な古墳|岩屋古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） * [[団原古墳]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]） * [[古宮古墳]]（[[大分県]][[大分市]]）
== ギャラリー == <gallery widths="180px" heights="150px"> 画像:Oni no Secchin.JPG|鬼の雪隠 File:観音塚古墳（羽曳野市）石室内部.JPG|観音塚古墳石室内部 画像:向山1号墳玄室.JPG|岩屋古墳石室内部 ファイル:Danbara kofun-sekishitsu.jpg|伝・団原古墳出土石室 </gallery>
== 参考文献 == * [[田村晃一]]・合田芳正（監修）『考古学探訪の基礎用語』[[山川出版社]]、141頁、2000年7月
== 関連項目 == * [[古墳]] * [[石棺]] * [[石室]] * [[益田岩船]] * [[終末期古墳]]
{{DEFAULTSORT:よこあなしきせつかく}} [[Category:考古遺跡に見る葬制]] [[Category:古墳]]

revision.comment: /* 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 */
revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。

主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。
== 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 == * [[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]） * [[牽牛子塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[高松塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[キトラ古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[中尾山古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[束明神古墳]]（奈良県高取町） * [[石のカラト古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]・[[京都府]][[木津川市]]） * [[シシヨツカ古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） * [[小口山古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） * [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[お亀石古墳]]（大阪府[[富田林市]]） * [[尾市古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[猪ノ子古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[北塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[向山古墳群#主な古墳|岩屋古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） * [[団原古墳]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]） * [[古宮古墳]]（[[大分県]][[大分市]]）

'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。

revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。
== 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 == * [[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]） * [[牽牛子塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[高松塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[キトラ古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[中尾山古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[束明神古墳]]（奈良県高取町） * [[石のカラト古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]・[[京都府]][[木津川市]]） * [[シシヨツカ古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） * [[小口山古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） * [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[お亀石古墳]]（大阪府[[富田林市]]） * [[尾市古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[猪ノ子古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[北塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[向山古墳群#主な古墳|岩屋古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） * [[団原古墳]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]） * [[古宮古墳]]（[[大分県]][[大分市]]）

'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。

revision.comment: /* ギャラリー */ 画像変更。
revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。
== 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 == * [[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]） * [[牽牛子塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[高松塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[キトラ古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[中尾山古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[束明神古墳]]（奈良県高取町） * [[石のカラト古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]・[[京都府]][[木津川市]]） * [[シシヨツカ古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） * [[小口山古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） * [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[お亀石古墳]]（大阪府[[富田林市]]） * [[尾市古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[猪ノ子古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[北塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[向山古墳群#主な古墳|岩屋古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） * [[団原古墳]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]） * [[古宮古墳]]（[[大分県]][[大分市]]）

revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。
== 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 == * [[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]） * [[牽牛子塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[高松塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[キトラ古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[中尾山古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[束明神古墳]]（奈良県高取町） * [[石のカラト古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]・[[京都府]][[木津川市]]） * [[シシヨツカ古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） * [[小口山古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） * [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[お亀石古墳]]（大阪府[[富田林市]]） * [[尾市古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[猪ノ子古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[北塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[向山古墳群#主な古墳|岩屋古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） * [[団原古墳]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]） * [[古宮古墳 (大分市)|古宮古墳]]（[[大分県]][[大分市]]）

revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。

切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。

'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。

revision.comment: Created page with "'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室). This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it incl..."
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Immanuelle moved page [[Yokoguchi-style stone coffin]] to [[Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku]]
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City {{ill|Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun|ja|石宝殿古墳}} Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳}} Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳}} (relocated) </gallery>
== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}, p.141, July 2000.

revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City {{ill|Ishinohōden Kofun|ja|石宝殿古墳}} Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino)|ja|観音塚古墳}} Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳}} (relocated) </gallery>
== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shuppansha|ja|山川出版社}}, p.141, July 2000.
== Related Topics == * {{ill|Kofun|ja|古墳}} * {{ill|Sarcophagus|ja|石棺}} * {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|石室}} * {{ill|Masuda-no-Iwafune|ja|益田岩船}} * {{ill|Terminal Kofun|ja|終末期古墳}}

'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.

revision.comment: /* Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers */
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''Ishikanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: doing a mass eplace to fix the Sekkan references since I mistakenly linked them to [[:en:Sarcophagus]] when it is a different topic
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: ([[c:GR|GR]]) [[c:COM:FR|File renamed]]: [[File:Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg]] → [[File:Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B1.jpg]] [[c:COM:FR#FR1|Criterion 1]] (original uploader’s request)
revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。
== 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 == * [[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]） * [[牽牛子塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[高松塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[キトラ古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[中尾山古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[束明神古墳]]（奈良県高取町） * [[石のカラト古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]・[[京都府]][[木津川市]]） * [[シシヨツカ古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） * [[小口山古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） * [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[お亀石古墳]]（大阪府[[富田林市]]） * [[尾市古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[猪ノ子古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[北塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[向山古墳群#主な古墳|岩屋古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） * [[団原古墳]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]） * [[古宮古墳 (大分市)|古宮古墳]]（[[大分県]][[大分市]]）

revision.comment: Moving from [[Category:Original pages]] to [[Category:unsorted link categories]][[category:unsorted completion categories]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.

revision.comment: Bumping for adding the Kofun template
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.comment: fixing spacing error
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.

'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.

[[ja:横口式石槨]]

revision.comment: /* ギャラリー */ 画像追加。
revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。
== 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 == * [[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]） * [[牽牛子塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[高松塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[キトラ古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[中尾山古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[束明神古墳]]（奈良県高取町） * [[石のカラト古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]・[[京都府]][[木津川市]]） * [[シシヨツカ古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） * [[小口山古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） * [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[お亀石古墳]]（大阪府[[富田林市]]） * [[尾市古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[猪ノ子古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[北塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[向山古墳群#主な古墳|岩屋古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） * [[団原古墳]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]） * [[古宮古墳 (大分市)|古宮古墳]]（[[大分県]][[大分市]]）
== ギャラリー == <gallery caption="大阪府"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|寝屋川市 [[石宝殿古墳]] Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|富田林市 [[お亀石古墳]] Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|柏原市 [[平尾山古墳群|平尾山古墳群平尾山第67支群2号墳]] Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|羽曳野市 [[オーコ8号墳]] Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|羽曳野市 [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]] Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B1.jpg|羽曳野市 [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]] Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|羽曳野市 [[小口山古墳]] Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|太子町 [[松井塚古墳]]（移築） Goryoyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|太子町 [[御嶺山古墳]] Tsukamari Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|河南町 [[ツカマリ古墳]] Shiraki Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|河南町 [[白木古墳]] </gallery> <gallery caption="奈良県"> Masuda-no-Iwafune, 3D-B.jpg|橿原市 [[益田岩船]] Hanayamazuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|桜井市 [[花山塚古墳]] Koseyama Kofun Group-323, 3D-B.jpg|御所市 [[巨勢山323号墳]] Terasaki Shirakabezuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|高取町 [[寺崎白壁塚古墳]] Hirano Tsukaanayama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|香芝市 [[平野塚穴山古墳]] Ueyama Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|平群町 [[上山2号墳]]（移築） Jindai Kofun, sekkaku.jpg|斑鳩町 [[神代古墳]] Tatsuta Goboyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|斑鳩町 [[竜田御坊山3号墳]] Takamatsuzuka Kofun, replica 3D-B.jpg|明日香村 [[高松塚古墳]]（レプリカ） Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin, 3D-B.jpg|明日香村 [[鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠]] Koshi Tsukagomon Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|明日香村 [[越塚御門古墳]] Kengoshizuka Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|明日香村 [[牽牛子塚古墳]] </gallery> <gallery caption="その他"> Yachikubo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|福島県白河市 [[谷地久保古墳]] Wakasano Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|兵庫県相生市 [[若狭野古墳]] Ishi-no-Hoden, 3D-B.jpg|兵庫県高砂市 [[石の宝殿]] Yamagahana Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|鳥取県鳥取市 [[山ヶ鼻古墳]] Megurihara Kofun Group-1, 3D-B.jpg|島根県松江市 [[廻原1号墳]] Nagasako Kofun Group-2, 3D-B.jpg|岡山県総社市 [[長砂2号墳]] Inoko Kofun, entrance.jpg|広島県福山市 [[猪ノ子古墳]] Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|広島県福山市 [[曽根田白塚古墳]] Oichi Kofun, 3D-B1-2.jpg|広島県福山市 [[尾市1号墳]] Miyajidake Kofun, 3D-1.jpg|福岡県福津市 [[宮地嶽古墳]] Tebika Namikirifudo Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|福岡県福津市 [[手光波切不動古墳]] Furumiya Kofun (Oita), 3D-B.jpg|大分県大分市 [[古宮古墳 (大分市)|古宮古墳]] </gallery>

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq-{{subst:PAGENAME}}-qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:石室]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:石室]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: linking [[Stone Chamber]]
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "|木棺}}" to "|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}}"
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.comment: Text replacement - "小口山古墳}}" to "小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}}"
revision.text:
'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Yokoguchi-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "Yokoguchi" to "Cave Tomb"
revision.text:
'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Text replacement - "q---qq" to "qqqqqqqqq"
revision.text:
'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.text:
'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Qq deprecated start]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City {{ill|Ishinohōden Kofun|ja|石宝殿古墳}} Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳}} (relocated) </gallery>

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Attempting to force refresh]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Unsorted completion categories]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:qq]][[en:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
