revision.comment: Text replacement - "qq" to "11"
revision.text:
'''Cave Tomb-style Sekkaku''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Attempting to force efresh]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.comment: moving the kofun template to the bottom of each page
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City {{ill|Ishinohōden Kofun|ja|石宝殿古墳}} Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳}} (relocated) </gallery>
== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shu11ansha|ja|山川出版社}}, p.141, July 2000.

revision.comment: Adding [[Category:return the see also]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City {{ill|Ishinohōden Kofun|ja|石宝殿古墳}} Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳}} (relocated) </gallery>

This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.

revision.comment: Removing from [[Category:Tier 1]] using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City {{ill|Ishinohōden Kofun|ja|石宝殿古墳}} Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳}} (relocated) </gallery>

revision.comment: Bot: Reformatted page - moved categories, interwikis, DEFAULTSORT, and removed duplicates.
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.comment: Copying from [[Category:Kofun template and pages that transclude it]] to [[Category:qq]][[sn:{{subst:PAGENAME}}]][[Category:qq]] on using [[c:Help:Cat-a-lot|Cat-a-lot]]
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=draft}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=draft}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳|12=draft}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=draft}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳|12=draft}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)|12=draft}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=draft}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=draft}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳|12=draft}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=draft}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳|12=draft}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)|12=draft}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.comment: fixing up the kofun templates
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=draft}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=draft}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳|12=draft}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=draft}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳|12=draft}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)|12=draft}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}.

'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|12=draft}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|12=draft}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=draft}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|12=draft}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|12=draft}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|12=draft}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|12=draft}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=draft}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳|12=draft}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=draft}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳|12=draft}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=draft}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=draft}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳|12=draft}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)|12=draft}} (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City {{ill|Ishinohōden Kofun|ja|石宝殿古墳|12=draft}} Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳|12=draft}} Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=draft}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun|12=draft}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|12=draft}} Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=draft}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=draft}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳|12=draft}} (relocated) </gallery>

revision.text:
'''横口式石槨'''（よこぐちしきせっかく）とは、後期～終末期の古墳にみられる横穴式墓制の一つである。'''石棺式石室'''とも言う。
切石を用いて、内部に[[木棺]]や[[乾漆棺]]を納められるよう程度の大きさに造られ、短辺の小口部が開口する。前室や[[羨道]]が付く場合もある。
主として[[畿内]]に分布し、[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]の[[観音塚古墳]]や[[奈良県]][[明日香村]]の[[高松塚古墳]]などが代表的な例。
== 石槨式石室をもつ主な古墳 == * [[鬼の爼・鬼の雪隠]]（[[奈良県]][[高市郡]][[明日香村]]） * [[牽牛子塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[高松塚古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[キトラ古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[中尾山古墳]]（奈良県高市郡明日香村） * [[束明神古墳]]（奈良県高取町） * [[石のカラト古墳]]（[[奈良県]][[奈良市]]・[[京都府]][[木津川市]]） * [[シシヨツカ古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[南河内郡]][[河南町]]） * [[小口山古墳]]（[[大阪府]][[羽曳野市]]） * [[鉢伏山西峰古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|観音塚古墳]]（大阪府羽曳野市） * [[お亀石古墳]]（大阪府[[富田林市]]） * [[尾市古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[猪ノ子古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[曽根田白塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） *[[北塚古墳]]（広島県[[福山市]]） * [[向山古墳群#主な古墳|岩屋古墳]]（[[鳥取県]][[米子市]]） * [[団原古墳]]（[[島根県]][[松江市]]） * [[古宮古墳 (大分市)|古宮古墳]]（[[大分県]][[大分市]]）

revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai region|qq=}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kengoshizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishinokarato Kofun|lt=Ishinokarato Kofun|lt=Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|qq=}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okameishi Kofun|qq=}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danbara Kofun|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun|lt=Furumiya Kofun|lt=Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|qq=}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.comment: Bot: fix redirect [[Takamiya Shrine Kofun]] → [[Tsukamyojin Kofun]]
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai region|ja|畿内|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kinai region|qq=}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Habikino, Osaka|ja|羽曳野市|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Habikino, Osaka|qq=}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|ja|明日香村|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Asuka, Nara|qq=}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|ja|牽牛子塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kengoshizuka Kofun|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Kofun|ja|高松塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamatsuzuka Kofun|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Tomb|lt=Kitora Kofun|ja|キトラ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitora Kofun|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|ja|中尾山古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Nakaoyama Kofun|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishinokarato Kofun|lt=Ishinokarato Kofun|lt=Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|ja|石のカラト古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|qq=}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okameishi Kofun|qq=}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|ja|団原古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Danbara Kofun|qq=}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun|lt=Furumiya Kofun|lt=Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|ja|古宮古墳 (大分市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|qq=}} (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City {{ill|Ishinohōden Kofun|ja|石宝殿古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ishinohōden Kofun|qq=}} Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|ja|お亀石古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Okameishi Kofun|qq=}} Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oko Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳 (羽曳野市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuizuka Kofun|qq=}} (relocated) </gallery>

revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; revision.comment: Bot: add category Category:古墳
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)

revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)

== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shu11ansha|ja|山川出版社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shu11ansha|qq=}}, p.141, July 2000. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|qq=|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Masuda-no-Iwafune|ja|益田岩船|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuda-no-Iwafune|qq=}} * {{ill|Terminal Kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terminal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}

revision.comment: Bot: remove deleted category Cave Tomb-style sekkaku
revision.text:
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Kanshitsu coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanshitsu coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City [[:en:Ishinohōden Kofun|Ishinohōden Kofun]] Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oko Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuizuka Kofun|qq=}} (relocated) </gallery>

Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18458609]] Wikidata name: stone sarcophagus with a side entrance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18458609]] Wikidata name: stone sarcophagus with a side entrance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City [[:en:Ishinohōden Kofun|Ishinohōden Kofun]] Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oko Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuizuka Kofun|qq=}} (relocated) </gallery>
== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shu11ansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shu11ansha|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, p.141, July 2000. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Masuda-no-Iwafune|ja|益田岩船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuda-no-Iwafune|qq=}} * {{ill|Terminal Kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terminal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18458609]] Wikidata name: stone sarcophagus with a side entrance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City [[:en:Ishinohōden Kofun|Ishinohōden Kofun]] Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oko Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuizuka Kofun|qq=}} (relocated) </gallery>
== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shu11ansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shu11ansha|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, p.141, July 2000. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Masuda-no-Iwafune|ja|益田岩船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuda-no-Iwafune|qq=}} * {{ill|Terminal Kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terminal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}

<!--Side-entrance chamber--><!--Side-entrance chamber--><!--Side-entrance chamber--> [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Archaeology of death]] <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q18458609}} [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]]

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18458609]] Wikidata name: stone sarcophagus with a side entrance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City [[:en:Ishinohōden Kofun|Ishinohōden Kofun]] Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oko Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuizuka Kofun|qq=}} (relocated) </gallery>
== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shu11ansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shu11ansha|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, p.141, July 2000. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Masuda-no-Iwafune|ja|益田岩船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuda-no-Iwafune|qq=}} * {{ill|Terminal Kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terminal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}
