revision.comment: Consolidate multiple wikidata link templates into single one at end
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18458609]] Wikidata name: stone sarcophagus with a side entrance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City [[:en:Ishinohōden Kofun|Ishinohōden Kofun]] Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oko Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuizuka Kofun|qq=}} (relocated) </gallery>

The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)

revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18458609]] Wikidata name: stone sarcophagus with a side entrance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18458609]] Wikidata name: stone sarcophagus with a side entrance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City [[:en:Ishinohōden Kofun|Ishinohōden Kofun]] Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oko Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuizuka Kofun|qq=}} (relocated) </gallery>
== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shu11ansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shu11ansha|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, p.141, July 2000. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Masuda-no-Iwafune|ja|益田岩船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuda-no-Iwafune|qq=}} * {{ill|Terminal Kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terminal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}

<!--Side-entrance chamber--><!--Side-entrance chamber--><!--Side-entrance chamber--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q18458609}} [[Category:Archaeology of death]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]] [[Category:pages with conflicting qids]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "pages with conflicting qids" to "qq"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18458609]] Wikidata name: stone sarcophagus with a side entrance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the [[:en:Kinai|Kinai region]], with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in [[:en:Habikino|Habikino, Osaka]], and the [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] in [[:en:Asuka, Nara|Asuka, Nara]].
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kengoshizuka Kofun|Kengoshizuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Takamatsuzuka Tomb|Takamatsuzuka Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Kitora Tomb|Kitora Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * [[:en:Nakaoyama Kofun|Nakaoyama Kofun]] (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * [[:en:Ishinokarato Kofun|Ishi-no-Karato Kofun]] (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] (Habikino, Osaka) * [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * [[:en:Danbara Kofun|Danbara Kofun]] (Matsue, Shimane) * [[:en:Furumiya Kofun|Furumiya Kofun (Oita)]] (Oita City, Oita)
== Gallery == <gallery caption="Osaka Prefecture"> Ishi-no-Hoden Kofun, 3D-B-2.jpg|Neyagawa City [[:en:Ishinohōden Kofun|Ishinohōden Kofun]] Okameishi Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Tondabayashi City [[:en:Okameishi Kofun|Okameishi Kofun]] Hiraoyama Kofun Group 67-2, 3D-B.jpg|Kashiwara City {{ill|Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hirao Mountain Kofun Cluster|lt=Hiraoyama Kofun Group|ja|平尾山古墳群|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hiraoyama Kofun Group|qq=}} Oko Kofun Group-8, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Oko Kofun Group|ja|オーコ8号墳|1=Ōko No. 8 Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oko Kofun Group|13=User:Immanuelle/Ōko No. 8 Kofun|qq=}} Kannonzuka Kofun (Habikino), 3D-B-2.jpg|Habikino City [[:en:Kannonzuka Kofun|Kannonzuka Kofun]] Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} Koguchiyama Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|Habikino City {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} Matsuizuka Kofun, sekkaku-1.jpg|Taishi Town {{ill|Matsuizuka Kofun|ja|松井塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsuizuka Kofun|qq=}} (relocated) </gallery>
== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shu11ansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shu11ansha|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, p.141, July 2000. == See Also == * [[:en:Kofun|Kofun]] * {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Masuda-no-Iwafune|ja|益田岩船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuda-no-Iwafune|qq=}} * {{ill|Terminal Kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terminal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}

== References == * Koichi Tamura, Yoshimasa Goto (Supervisors), "Fundamental Terminology for Archaeological Explorations," {{ill|Yamakawa Shu11ansha|ja|山川出版社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yamakawa Shu11ansha|1=Yamakawa Shuppansha|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11468274}}, p.141, July 2000. == See Also == * {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|WD=Q1141225|lt=Kofun}} * {{ill|sekkan|ja|石棺|1=Sekkan|1=Sekkan (Sarcophagus)|lt=Sarcophagus|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/sekkan|13=User:Immanuelle/Sekkan|qq=}} * {{ill|Stone chamber|ja|石室|1=Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Stone Chamber|qq=}} * {{ill|Masuda-no-Iwafune|ja|益田岩船|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masuda-no-Iwafune|qq=}} * {{ill|Terminal Kofun|ja|終末期古墳|1=Late Kofun tumuli|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Terminal Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Late Kofun tumuli|qq=}}

<!--Side-entrance chamber--><!--Side-entrance chamber--><!--Side-entrance chamber--> <!--jawiki derived wikidata interwikis--> {{wikidata link|Q18458609}} [[Category:Archaeology of death]] [[Category:Completion tier 1]] [[Category:Kofun]] [[Category:Kofun (general concept)]] [[Category:Wikidata has English label]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]

revision.comment: Text replacement - "[[Category:Wikidata has English label]]" to "[[Category:qq]]"
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] {{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q18458609]] Wikidata name: stone sarcophagus with a side entrance [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:30, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} '''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|en|Kengoshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11571102|lt=Kengoshizuka Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|en|Nakaoyama Kofun|WD=Q11363940|lt=Nakaoyama Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishinokarato Kofun|en|Ishinokarato Kofun|WD=Q11584729|lt=Ishi-no-Karato Kofun}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|en|Kannonzuka Kofun|WD=Q11630595|lt=Kannonzuka Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|en|Okameishi Kofun|WD=Q11262874|lt=Okameishi Kofun}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|en|Danbara Kofun|WD=Q5214944|lt=Danbara Kofun}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun|en|Furumiya Kofun|WD=Q11411029|lt=Furumiya Kofun (Oita)}} (Oita City, Oita)

revision.comment: imported only latest revision from https://archive.org/details/miraheze-wiki-shintowiki-20251218 to avoid excessive disk usage, comment on the revision was Removing {{afc comment}} templates
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:横口式石槨]]
'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|en|Kengoshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11571102|lt=Kengoshizuka Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|en|Nakaoyama Kofun|WD=Q11363940|lt=Nakaoyama Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishinokarato Kofun|en|Ishinokarato Kofun|WD=Q11584729|lt=Ishi-no-Karato Kofun}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|en|Kannonzuka Kofun|WD=Q11630595|lt=Kannonzuka Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|en|Okameishi Kofun|WD=Q11262874|lt=Okameishi Kofun}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|en|Danbara Kofun|WD=Q5214944|lt=Danbara Kofun}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun|en|Furumiya Kofun|WD=Q11411029|lt=Furumiya Kofun (Oita)}} (Oita City, Oita)

'''Side-entrance Sarcophagus Chamber''' (横口式石槨) refers to a type of horizontal stone burial structure observed in the later to terminal phases of the Kofun period. It is also known as '''sekkanshiki Sekishitsu''' (石棺式石室).
This type of burial chamber is constructed using cut stones, designed to house {{ill|wooden coffins|ja|木棺|1=Wooden Coffin|1=Wooden Coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/wooden coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Wooden Coffin|qq=}} or {{ill|dry lacquer coffins|ja|乾漆棺|1=Dry-lacquer coffin|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/dry lacquer coffins|13=User:Immanuelle/Dry-lacquer coffin|qq=}}. The chamber features an opening on one of its shorter sides. In some cases, it includes an antechamber or {{ill|Endō|lt=passageway (sen-dō)|ja|羨道|1=endō|lt=endō (tomb entrance)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Endō|13=User:Immanuelle/endō|qq=}}.
The distribution of such burial structures is primarily concentrated in the {{ill|Kinai|en|Kinai|WD=Q906708|lt=Kinai region|1=Kinai}}, with notable examples including the {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|ja|観音塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kannonzuka Kofun|qq=}} in {{ill|Habikino|en|Habikino|WD=Q694308|lt=Habikino, Osaka|1=Habikino}}, and the {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun|1=Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} in {{ill|Asuka, Nara|en|Asuka, Nara|WD=Q752397|lt=Asuka, Nara|1=Asuka, Nara}}.
== Major Kofun with Sekkaku-style Burial Chambers == * {{ill|Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|ja|鬼の俎・鬼の雪隠|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oni-no-Manaita and Oni-no-Secchin|qq=}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kengoshizuka Kofun|en|Kengoshizuka Kofun|WD=Q11571102|lt=Kengoshizuka Kofun|1=Kengoshizuka Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|en|Takamatsuzuka Tomb|WD=Q1039675|lt=Takamatsuzuka Kofun|1=Takamatsuzuka Tomb}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Kitora Tomb|en|Kitora Tomb|WD=Q846393|lt=Kitora Kofun|1=Kitora Tomb}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Nakaoyama Kofun|en|Nakaoyama Kofun|WD=Q11363940|lt=Nakaoyama Kofun|1=Nakaoyama Kofun}} (Asuka, Nara) * {{ill|Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Tsukamyojin Kofun|lt=Takamiya Shrine Kofun|ja|束明神古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takamiya Shrine Kofun|qq=}} (Takatori, Nara) * {{ill|Ishinokarato Kofun|en|Ishinokarato Kofun|WD=Q11584729|lt=Ishi-no-Karato Kofun|1=Ishinokarato Kofun}} (Nara City, Nara; Kizugawa, Kyoto) * {{ill|Shishiyotsuka Kofun|ja|シシヨツカ古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shishiyotsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Kanan, Osaka) * {{ill|Koguchiyama Kofun|ja|小口山古墳|1=Koguchiyama Kofun|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|13=User:Immanuelle/Koguchiyama Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibuseyama Nishimine Kofun|lt=Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|ja|鉢伏山西峰古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Hachibusuyama Nishimine Kofun|qq=}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Kannonzuka Kofun|en|Kannonzuka Kofun|WD=Q11630595|lt=Kannonzuka Kofun|1=Kannonzuka Kofun}} (Habikino, Osaka) * {{ill|Okameishi Kofun|en|Okameishi Kofun|WD=Q11262874|lt=Okameishi Kofun|1=Okameishi Kofun}} (Tondabayashi, Osaka) * {{ill|Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja|尾市古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Oichi No.1 Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 尾市1号墳}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Inoko Kofun|ja|猪ノ子古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Inoko Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|ja|曽根田白塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Soneda Shiratsuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Kitazuka Kofun|ja|北塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kitazuka Kofun|qq=}} (Fukuyama, Hiroshima) * {{ill|Iwaya Kofun|ja|岩屋古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Iwaya Kofun|qq=}} (Yonago, Tottori) * {{ill|Danbara Kofun|en|Danbara Kofun|WD=Q5214944|lt=Danbara Kofun|1=Danbara Kofun}} (Matsue, Shimane) * {{ill|Furumiya Kofun|en|Furumiya Kofun|WD=Q11411029|lt=Furumiya Kofun (Oita)|1=Furumiya Kofun}} (Oita City, Oita)
